pi_items.source: Direct HTTP verification: curl -I https://books.toscrape.com/ returns HTTP/2 200. Web search confirms it is a real, publicly accessible e-commerce sandbox site designed for practicing web scraping, owned and maintained by toscrape.com.

Code analysis: export_result.sh line 12 hardcodes OUTPUT_FILE=/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/site_graph.html; setup_task.sh lines 29-32 create directory /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports and delete any existing site_graph.html files before task execution.

pi_items.verified_value: Minimum file size requirement is > 1024 bytes (strictly greater than), not >= 1024
pi_items.source: Code analysis: export_result.sh line 37 checks 'if [ "$FILE_SIZE" -gt 1024 ]' (-gt means greater than, not greater-than-or-equal). Comment on line 38 says 'Arbitrary small size check', confirming 1024 is just a threshold value. The actual requirement is that files must be larger than 1024 bytes, meaning minimum acceptable size is 1025 bytes.

Code analysis: task.json description line 5 states 'Configure the spider to limit the crawl to 200 URLs'; setup_task.sh line 81 instructs 'Set Crawl Limit to 200 (Config > Spider > Limits)'. The task requires the agent to manually set this limit in the UI.

privileged_info_summary: The task crawls https://books.toscrape.com/, a real, publicly accessible e-commerce sandbox website specifically designed for web scraping practice. The target website responds with HTTP 200 and contains valid HTML content. The agent must use Screaming Frog SEO Spider to crawl the site with a 200 URL limit, generate a force-directed graph visualization, and export it as an HTML file to /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/site_graph.html. The exported HTML file must be larger than 1024 bytes (not exactly 1024) and contain HTML structure plus the target domain name 'books.toscrape.com'. All metadata claims are verified as correct except for the min_file_size_bytes claim, which states 1024 but the actual requirement in the code is strictly greater than 1024 (minimum 1025 bytes).
